title: Today's AI Brief: Open-Source Coding Models and Microsoft's Advanced Reasoning AI
summary: A look at Nous Research's open-source coding model and Microsoft's new advanced reasoning AI.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're covering some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, Nous Research has released an open-source coding model called NousCoder-14B. This model is making waves because it's competitive with larger proprietary models, and it's open-source, which could shake up the coding landscape. I mean, who doesn't love a good underdog story?

Nous Research, backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, claims that NousCoder-14B matches or exceeds several larger proprietary models. This is significant because it shows that open-source models can be just as effective as their proprietary counterparts. It's like the David vs. Goliath story, but in this case, David is getting a lot bigger. And who knows, maybe we'll see more open-source models popping up in the future.

Next, Microsoft has announced its first advanced reasoning AI model, called MAI-Thinking-1. This model is one of seven new models announced at Build 2026. What's interesting here is that Microsoft is focusing on advanced reasoning, which could have significant implications for areas like problem-solving and decision-making. I wonder what kind of problems this model will be able to solve that current AI models can't.

Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment is also worth a quick mention. My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
